post.description:
Get persona information using two-pass architecture.

This is a thin HTTP wrapper around get_persona_internal().

Query 1: Access check (user role, departments, persona state)
Query 2: ID fetching (resource IDs, suggestions, agents)
Pass 2: Parallel resource fetching (each resource type has own cache)
Get personas list with permissions and scenario details.
Save persona - handles both create (persona_id = NULL) and update (persona_id provided).
Duplicate a persona.
Delete a persona.

patch.description: Patch persona draft - accepts resource IDs and creates/updates draft.

post.description:
Get comprehensive persona documentation with dynamic schema and business logic.

Returns:
    Complete documentation including:
    - Database schema (columns, foreign keys) from information_schema
    - Junction tables and relationships
    - Business logic extracted from permissions.py
    - API routing information
    - GLOW context and use cases

schema.title: Response Get Persona Docs Api V4 Personas Docs Post

post.description:
Get scenario information using two-pass architecture.

Query 1: Access check (user role, departments, scenario state)
Query 2: ID fetching (resource IDs, suggestions, agents)
Pass 2: Parallel resource fetching (each resource type has own cache)
Get scenarios list with all relationships.
Save scenario - handles both create (input_scenario_id = NULL) and update (input_scenario_id provided).
Duplicate a scenario.
Delete a scenario.

patch.description: Patch scenario draft - accepts resource IDs and creates/updates draft.

post.description:
Get simulation information using two-pass architecture.

Query 1: Access check (user role, departments, simulation state)
Query 2: ID fetching (resource IDs, suggestions, agents)
Pass 2: Parallel resource fetching (each resource type has own cache)
Get simulations list with SQL-computed permissions.

Save simulation - handles both create (input_simulation_id = NULL) and update (input_simulation_id provided).

Uses two-pass architecture:
1. Check access and permissions in Python
2. Execute save if permitted

Duplicate a simulation.

Uses two-pass architecture:
1. Check access and permissions in Python
2. Execute duplicate if permitted

Delete a simulation (with usage check).

Uses two-pass architecture:
1. Check access and permissions in Python
2. Execute delete if permitted

patch.description:
Patch simulation draft - accepts resource IDs and creates/updates draft.

Uses Python permission checks before executing draft operation.

Validation Logic:
- Tools are REQUIRED for resources - error if no tools exist (via missing_tools_check CTE)
- Agents are OPTIONAL - NULL agent_id means manual entry only (no generate button shown)
- Frontend components check agent_id before showing generate button

post.description: Get detailed group information with all runs and messages.
Get paginated group list with resource hydration.
Get session detail with paginated audits and groups.
Get paginated session list with resource hydration.

Get simulations available for training (operational).

OPERATIONAL endpoint: Returns simulations user can take, scoped by
their cohorts based on practice mode.

Used by frontend to display available simulations and get data
needed to start a training session (scenario_ids, etc).

Refresh all training section materialized views.

Uses SQL function that refreshes MVs in dependency order:
1. mv_home_chat_facts (base fact table)
2. mv_home_simulation_status (depends on chat_facts)
3. mv_home_attempt_history (depends on chat_facts)
4. mv_home_certificate_status (depends on chat_facts)

Get attempt detail with parallel MV fetching.

Uses view internal handlers with pool-based parallel fetch:
- View 1: simulation_attempts (attempt-level aggregates)
- View 2: simulation_chats (chat-level data with grades/feedbacks)
- View 3: simulation_messages (message-level data with strengths/improvements/hints)

Each query runs on its own connection from the pool for true parallelism.
The practice flag is determined from the attempt data itself.
Get unified attempt history list for home/practice style screens.
Bulk archive or unarchive attempts (simulation or benchmark).
Generate a certificate PDF/text for a profile.

Get dashboard artifact data.

Fetches analytics MV slices in parallel and assembles dashboard bundle.
Business calculations are performed in permissions.py.
Refresh all dashboard section materialized views.

Get reports artifact data.

Pulls four analytics MV slices in parallel and computes section skeletons.
Refresh all reports section materialized views.

Get leaderboard artifact data.

Pulls four analytics MV slices in parallel and computes leaderboard
section skeletons.
Refresh all leaderboard section materialized views.

Refresh all health section materialized views.

Refresh all benchmark section materialized views.

Get simulation attempt data from the materialized view.

This endpoint fetches attempt-level data with resource metadata JOINed.

Refresh the mv_simulation_attempts materialized view concurrently.

This is a fast refresh that doesn't require any schema changes.
It requires a unique index on the MV (mv_simulation_attempts_pk).

Use this when:
- You want to update the MV data without downtime
- The MV schema hasn't changed

Use /recreate when:
- You've changed the MV definition (schema changes)

Recreate the mv_simulation_attempts materialized view.

This drops and recreates the MV from its definition file.
There will be brief downtime while the MV is being recreated.

Use this when:
- You've changed the MV definition (schema changes)
- You need to rebuild from scratch

Use /refresh when:
- You just want to update the data
- The MV schema hasn't changed

Get simulation chat data from the materialized view.

This endpoint fetches chat-level data with resource metadata JOINed.
Refresh the mv_simulation_chats materialized view concurrently.
Recreate the mv_simulation_chats materialized view.

Get simulation message data from the materialized view.

This endpoint fetches message-level data with strengths/improvements.
Refresh the mv_simulation_messages materialized view concurrently.
Recreate the mv_simulation_messages materialized view.

Get attempt facts data from mv_attempt_facts.

This endpoint fetches paginated attempt-level data with:
- Filtering (profile, attempt_type, archived, simulation, cohort, department, scenario, date, infinite_mode)
- Sorting (date, score)
- Pagination
- Filter options (simulation_options, scenario_options, profile_options)

Resource metadata (names, colors, icons) should be fetched separately
via internal resource handlers using the returned IDs.

schema.title: Response Persona Generation Error Api Socket V4 Server Persona Generation Error Post
Response Openid Configuration Default Idp  Well Known Openid Configuration Get

get.description: Authorization endpoint - handles Keycloak broker redirects.

post.description: Token endpoint - exchanges authorization codes for tokens.

schema.title: Response Token Default Idp Token Post

get.description: UserInfo endpoint - returns user claims from access token.

schema.title: Response Userinfo Default Idp Userinfo Get

get.description: JWKS endpoint for public key exposure.

RFC 8414 OAuth Authorization Server Metadata endpoint.

ChatGPT Dev Mode uses this endpoint to discover OAuth configuration.
This is required for OAuth to work with ChatGPT.

Rich health endpoint for dashboard.

Returns per-service status + latencies.
Automatically logs health checks to database when called by notify service.

post.description:
Trigger metrics snapshot to database.

Called by notify service to log metrics snapshot.
No leader election needed since notify service is single instance.

Trigger system initialization (SQL compilation and Keycloak sync).

Called by notify service to initialize system on startup.
Performs:
1. SQL type compilation (if types.py is missing or incomplete)
2. Keycloak sync to ensure identity providers are configured.

No authentication required - internal service-to-service call.

StrengthItem.description: Strength feedback for a message (id/message_id implied by parent).

TestJoinPayload.title: TestJoinPayload
TestJoinPayload.description: Request payload for test_join WebSocket event.

TestLeavePayload.title: TestLeavePayload
TestLeavePayload.description: Request payload for test_leave WebSocket event.

TestListFilterOption.title: TestListFilterOption
TestListFilterOption.description: Filter option row for tests list.

TestSendPayload.title: TestSendPayload
TestSendPayload.description: Request payload for test_send WebSocket event.

TestStopPayload.title: TestStopPayload
TestStopPayload.description: Request payload for test_stop WebSocket event.

TrainingErrorEvent.title: TrainingErrorEvent
TrainingErrorEvent.description:
Server-to-client event: training_error.

Emitted when an error occurs during training generation.

TrainingProgressEvent.title: TrainingProgressEvent
TrainingProgressEvent.description:
Server-to-client event: training_progress.

Optional - only sent if generation is happening. Client may not receive
any progress events if scenario already has content.

TrainingSimulationOperational.title: TrainingSimulationOperational
TrainingSimulationOperational.description:
Simulation data for starting a training session.

Contains data needed to start a simulation AND card display stats.
Now serves as the unified type for home/practice simulation cards.

TrainingStartPayload.title: TrainingStartPayload
TrainingStartPayload.description:
Request payload for training_start WebSocket event.

Starts a new training session. Server handles everything internally:
- Checks if scenario needs generation
- If generation needed, runs it and streams progress
- Creates attempt + chat entries
- Emits training_started when ready

TrainingStartedEvent.title: TrainingStartedEvent
TrainingStartedEvent.description:
Server-to-client event: training_started.

ALWAYS sent when training session is ready. This is the only success event
the client needs to handle. May be sent:
- Immediately if no generation was needed
- After generation completes if generation was needed
